symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly worsening ascites refractory to previously effective diuretics

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of diuretic response points to a new mechanical/space-occupying or malignant vascular driver.
Malignant portal vein occlusion produces rapidly worsening, diuretic-resistant ascites.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Simple cirrhotic decompensation without a new lesion
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Benign progression rarely becomes abruptly refractory to previously effective high-dose diuretics.

symptoms.symptom_name: Significant unintentional weight loss with cachexia (~10 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constant localised hepatic pain suggests capsular stretch from an expanding lesion or impending rupture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Jaundice (yellowing of eyes and skin) reported/observed on video

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hepatocellular carcinoma on cirrhosis with decompensation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reflects worsening hepatic synthetic/excretory failure; patient-observable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Anorexia, early satiety, inability to maintain oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Malignant cachexia/anorexia and mass effect; inability to hydrate also drives acute deterioration.
Marker of systemic decline and acuity; drives the emergency disposition.

symptoms.symptom_name: Breathlessness from tense ascites, eased by sitting upright

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Tense ascites causing restrictive respiratory compromise
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diaphragmatic splinting; a driver of the urgent disposition and the need for therapeutic paracentesis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known established cirrhosis from chronic hepatitis C (baseline risk state)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cirrhosis (especially HCV-related) is the dominant risk state for HCC and reframes any new hepatic feature as malignancy-until-excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hard, irregular, markedly enlarged liver edge extending ~5 cm below the costal margin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hard irregular hepatomegaly strongly suggests a hepatic malignancy — but it is a clinician palpation finding, sealed; the AI infers from history, it cannot palpate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hepatic bruit auscultated over the right upper quadrant

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hepatic bruit reflects tumour hypervascularity; clinician-only auscultation, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Shifting dullness / gross tense ascites on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician confirmation and quantification of ascites — sealed; the patient reports the swelling, the clinician confirms the sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals: tachycardia (HR ~102), tachypnoea (RR ~22), BP 105/65, Temp 37.1, SpO2 96%

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute hepatic decompensation with respiratory compromise
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective clinician measurement (no fever argues against SBP as sole driver); sealed — the AI cannot measure vitals without a device.

investigation_details.expected_result: markedly elevated (tumour marker) — expected, hospital-obtained; never a real-time telehealth value
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed serological support for HCC — must be obtained in hospital alongside imaging.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A markedly elevated AFP supports HCC; sealed hospital investigation, never available in real time on chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypervascular hepatic mass with arterial enhancement and washout on multiphase contrast CT/MRI

investigation_details.investigation_name: Multiphase contrast-enhanced CT or MRI of the liver
investigation_details.expected_result: solid hypervascular lesion with arterial hyperenhancement and portal-venous washout (± portal vein invasion) — diagnostic imaging pattern
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: No definitive diagnosis or staging; therapeutic paracentesis and oncology planning cannot proceed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Arterial hyperenhancement with washout in a cirrhotic liver is diagnostic of HCC (LI-RADS); requires imaging — impossible via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: HCC decompensation alarm triad in a cirrhotic
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Rapidly refractory ascites + significant unintentional weight loss + new localised hepatic pain in a known cirrhotic is the hallmark alarm constellation for an expanding HCC and mandates urgent imaging.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent multiphase contrast-enhanced CT (or MRI) of the liver
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive characterisation of the hepatic lesion and portal vein — hospital-based; the AI arranges emergency assessment, it does not image.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Diagnostic and therapeutic ascitic tap (paracentesis) with fluid analysis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes spontaneous bacterial peritonitis and relieves the tense ascites/respiratory compromise; a hospital procedure.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum AFP, LFTs, FBC, coagulation profile, renal function/electrolytes
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Tumour marker plus synthetic/renal/coagulation status to guide management; venous bloods are not obtainable in telehealth.
